content.problemStatement:
Chef is stuck in a two dimensional maze having **N** rows and **M** columns. He needs to get out of the maze as soon as possible and arrive at the kitchen in order to serve his hungry customers. But, he can get out of the maze only if he is able to successfully find any magical path in the given maze.



A path is defined as magical if it starts from any of the cell **(a,b)** of the maze and ends at the cell **(c,d)** such that the following conditions are satisfied :-


**|a - c| + |b - d| = 1**
All the cells in the maze are traversed exactly once.
It is allowed to move only in the four directions - up, down, left and right from the current cell.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two integers N and M, the dimensions of the maze.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output 'Yes' if a magical path exists, otherwise output 'No'.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The maze has 2 rows and 2 columns. We can start from cell (1, 1) and move down to cell (2, 1), then move right to cell (2, 2), and finally move up to cell (1, 2). This path visits all cells exactly once and satisfies the condition |a - c| + |b - d| = 1.

The maze has 3 rows and 3 columns. We can start from cell (1, 1) and move down to cell (2, 1), then move right to cell (2, 2), then move down to cell (3, 2), then move right to cell (3, 3), then move up to cell (2, 3), then move up to cell (1, 3), and finally move left to cell (1, 2). This path visits all cells exactly once and satisfies the condition |a - c| + |b - d| = 1.

solution.approach: To determine if a magical path exists, we need to check if the sum of the absolute differences of the corresponding x and y dimensions is 1. We can start from any cell (a, b) and try to find a path to cell (c, d) such that |a - c| + |b - d| = 1. We can use a breadth-first search (BFS) algorithm to traverse the maze and check if all cells are visited exactly once.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * (N + M)) — the time complexity is linear with respect to the number of test cases and the dimensions of the maze.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the space complexity is constant as we only need to store the dimensions of the maze and the result.
